questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Consider the following mechanism of an aldol condensation reaction:
Answer the following questions based on the above mechanism and your knowledge about aldol condensation.Question: If all the alpha hydrogen in $X$ is replaced by deuterium "D" atom. Which of the following would be observed? (Assuming high concentration of base)
options: Rate of reaction would decrease since $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{D}$ bond is stronger than $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{H}$ bond.
Rate of reaction would increase since D is more acidic than H .
Rate of reaction would remains same since C - D bond is not broken in the rate determining step.
III would be formed more easily than IV.
questions.solution: $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{H}$ bond is broken in non rate determining step, therefore, substitution of $\alpha-\mathrm{H}$ by deuterium doesn't affect the rate of reaction.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Consider the following mechanism of an aldol condensation reaction:
Answer the following questions based on the above mechanism and your knowledge about aldol condensation.Question: Which of the following statement regarding enolates I and II is true?
options: Enolate I is more stable than II, since it is formed by deprotonation of more acidic hydrogen.
Enolate I is more stable than II, since it is less substituted.
Enolate II is more stable than I, since it is formed by deprotonation of more stable than acidic hydrogen.
Enolate II is more stable than I, since it is more substituted.
questions.solution: More the number of alkyl substitute at double bond, greater its thermodynamic stability.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: An organic compound $(X)$ is subjected to reductive ozonolysis to give a product $(A)$ which on heating with dil. NaOH gives another product (B). Compound (B) on heating with sodium hypoiodite followed by acidification produces $(\mathrm{C})$. Compound $(\mathrm{C})$ on treatment with $\mathrm{SOCl}_2$ and then with diazomethane followed by hydrolysis gives a product which on heating gives 1, 2-dimethyl cyclopentene. Now answer the following questions.Question: Intermediate species formed in the reaction of (C) with $\mathrm{SOCl}_2 / \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{~N}_2 / \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} / \Delta$ is:
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: An organic compound $(X)$ is subjected to reductive ozonolysis to give a product ( $A$ ) which on heating with dil. NaOH gives another product (B). Compound (B) on heating with sodium hypoiodite followed by acidification produces (C). Compound (C) on treatment with $\mathrm{SOCl}_2$ and then with diazomethane followed by hydrolysis gives a product which on heating gives 1, 2-dimethyl cyclopentene. Now answer the following questions.Question: Organic compound $(X)$ in the above passage is:
